title: WDVX Blue Plate Special
description: Free live music every weekday at noon in the heart of downtown. A Knoxville institution featuring bluegrass, folk, country, and Americana artists.

title: Market Square Farmers Market
description: Vibrant outdoor market with local produce, artisan goods, street food, and live music every Saturday morning.

title: Ijams Nature Centre
description: Beautiful riverside nature preserve with hiking trails, rock climbing, kayak launches, and wildlife. A green oasis minutes from downtown.

title: Lady Vols Basketball at Thompson-Boling
description: Catch the legendary Tennessee Lady Vols women's basketball team at one of the best venues in college hoops.

title: First Friday Art Walk
description: Monthly celebration of local art, galleries, studios, and street performers. Galleries stay open late and many offer free wine.

title: Tennessee Theatre
description: Stunning 1920s movie palace turned performing arts venue. Catch Broadway shows, films, concerts, and dance in a stunning historic setting.

title: Cotton Eyed Joe
description: Legendary country bar and dance hall. Line dancing, live music, and mechanical bull. The biggest honky tonk in East Tennessee.

title: Pilot Light
description: Intimate DIY music venue in the Old City. Catch indie, punk, experimental, and underground acts in a no-frills space with great energy.

title: Big Ears Festival
description: World-class multi-genre music festival bringing experimental, jazz, classical, and avant-garde artists to intimate historic venues downtown.

title: Dogwood Arts Festival
description: Month-long spring celebration featuring art shows, trail of dogwood blooms, live music, and community events throughout Knoxville.

title: Old City Jazz at Preservation Pub
description: Multi-story bar with rooftop patio featuring live music almost every night. Famous for local and touring jazz, blues, and folk acts.

title: Clingman's Dome Sunrise Hike
description: Hike to the highest point in the Smokies for a breathtaking sunrise above the clouds. Plan for a 2am departure from Knoxville.

title: Sunsphere & World's Fair Park
description: Iconic gold globe from the 1982 World's Fair with a free observation deck and sweeping city views. Surrounded by a beautiful park.

title: Market Square Summer Concert Series
description: Free outdoor concerts every Friday evening in summer on Market Square. Local and regional acts, great food trucks, family atmosphere.

title: Silent Disco at Preservation Pub
description: Put on wireless headphones and choose your channel — three DJs, one dance floor, zero speakers. Wild, hilarious, and totally addictive.

title: Honky Tonk Thursday at International
description: $5 cover, cheap beer, and two-step lessons starting at 9pm every Thursday. The real country scene, no tourists.

title: Jazz at the Mill & Mine Lounge
description: Intimate jazz sessions in the lounge of the Mill & Mine on select Wednesday nights. Rotating local jazz trio, craft cocktails, low-key crowd.

title: Underground Rave Circuit — Old City
description: Rotating underground electronic music events in warehouses and bars around the Old City. Watch local Instagram pages for location reveals 24hrs before.

title: Rooftop Yoga at The Oliver Hotel
description: Sunday morning yoga on the rooftop terrace of the Oliver Hotel with city views. $15, BYOM (mat). Sign up via The Oliver website.

title: Sunday Bluegrass Jam — Suttree Landing
description: Informal bluegrass jam session in the park every Sunday afternoon. Bring an instrument or just listen. BYOB, all skill levels welcome.

title: Midnight Ramble — Market Square
description: Late-night acoustic sessions that pop up randomly around Market Square at midnight on warm summer weekends. Follow @midnightrambleknox.

title: Appalachian Rollergirls — Roller Derby
description: East Tennessee's flat-track roller derby league hosts bouts monthly. Loud, chaotic, and incredibly fun. Very affordable, very local.

title: Secret Cinema Club — Old City
description: Monthly underground film screening series showing cult classics, foreign films, and experimental cinema. Location revealed 48hrs before via newsletter.

title: Full Moon Drum Circle — Sequoyah Hills
description: Community drum circle and bonfire gathering on the night of every full moon in Sequoyah Hills Park. Free, open, eclectic crowd.
